summary: A small landlocked Balkan NATO member that resolved a decades-long name dispute with Greece in 2018, opening its path toward the EU while balancing Albanian-minority politics.

history.detail: The Ottoman region is split among Greece, Serbia and Bulgaria.
Becomes a constituent republic of socialist Yugoslavia.
Brief conflict ends with the Ohrid Agreement expanding minority rights.
Renames the country 'North Macedonia', ending the dispute with Greece.

enemies_rivals: historical frictions with Bulgaria over identity

finance.currency_role: Macedonian denar (MKD); de facto pegged to the euro
finance.central_bank: National Bank of the Republic of North Macedonia

gov_tech_links: Fund for Innovation and Technology Development

resources.water: Lake Ohrid and Vardar river; moderate supply

follow_the_money.detail: Auto-parts firms in free zones drive exports and jobs

theories_disputed.detail: Bulgaria blocks EU progress over language and history, claiming Macedonian identity is Bulgarian-derived.

connections.detail: Name dispute resolved by the 2018 Prespa Agreement
Identity and history disputes stall EU accession
Large Albanian minority links the two via Ohrid power-sharing
